sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 20.5 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.20)

statement.latex: (Well-known problem). Let$G$be a finite group, and$( G)$the group of normalized units of the integral group ring of~$G$. Do the spectra of$G$and$( G)$coincide? That is, is it true that, for any integer$n$, there is an element of order$n$in$( G)$if and only if there is an element of order$n$in$G$? The answer is ``yes'' if$G$is solvable (M.\,Hertweck, 36 (2008), 3585--3588). { A.\,B{\"a}chle, L.\,Ma...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
